T0287.title: Parachute Funds
T0287.definition: Air-dropped funds are illicit proceeds obtained by directly defrauding victims through methods like adult-content lead generation or fake task scams.
T0287.description: This is the black market term for money obtained from romance or sextortion scams. Fraud rings use social apps to post adult-content bait, tricking victims into transferring money under the guise of advance deposits, membership fees, or security deposits. These funds come directly from the victims' accounts, making the transaction chain relatively simple but involving numerous personal transfer records. Black market operators need a large number of personal payment codes or accounts to receive these air-dropped funds in a decentralized manner, thereby evading bank risk controls and police tracking.
T0287.usageExample: Air-dropped funds need to be processed through personal merchant codes; the exchange rate is very high.

references.title: Exposing Fraud-Laundering Models: Order-Posting Recharge Laundering and Merchant Laundering Spread Rapidly
The Current Card Vendor Ecosystem Under the Card-Crackdown Campaign
